english: 1. v. rań-ba (rang ba). -- 2. nyin-rańs-par (nyin rangs par) for to-rańs-kyi (tho rangs kyi) dús-su (dus su) early in the morning Tar. 111, 17. -- 3. in W. for réńs-pa (rengs pa).

1. vb. and adj. to keep, or keeping, the proper mean, to be proportionate, just right, adv. rán-par (ran par) moderately, rán-par (ran par) sro (sro) warm yourself moderately (tolerably) Lt., zas-tsód (zas tshod) rán-par (ran par) zá-ba (za ba) to eat moderately S.g.; ₒdi-tsam ('di tsam) ni (ni) rán-no (ran no) this is about the proper measure Dzl.; with the root of the vb.: žiń (zhing) rńá-ran-nas (rnga ran nas) as it was (the proper) time for harvest Dzl., ₒgro-ran ('gro ran) it is time to go Pth., śi (shi) ma (ma) rán-par (ran par) śi-ba (shi ba) to die an untimely death; bág-mar (bag mar) gtań-rán-pa (gtang ran pa) dań (dang) when it was time to give her in marriage Dzl.; not so often with a sbst.: rtsás-ma (rtsas ma) rán-tsa-na (ran tsa na) when harvest-time had come Mil. -- 2. rtsa (rtsa) rán-pa (ran pa) C. shave-grass, Equisetum arvense. -- 3. col. for ₒdrén-pa ('dren pa) to lead (water); for bran-pa (bran pa) v. ťags (thags).

I. superior, excellent; the eldest, of three sons, opp. to briń-po (bring po) and ťa-čuń (tha chung), frq.; gań-zág (gang zag) dbań-po-ráb-rnams (dbang po rab rnams) very able or clever persons (opp. to briń-po (bring po) or ťá-ma (tha ma) having moderate or very little capacity) Mil., Thgr., inst. of which rab (rab) ₒbriń ('bring) ysum (gsum) is often used Thgy.; t'ébs-na (thebs na) rab (rab) if rightly understood, that will be the best Thgr., frq. for: so it is right, that will do; much, plentiful, rab-skrái (rab skrai) ₒóg-nas ('og nas) also with a full head of hair (you may be a holy man) Mil.; ráb-tu (rab tu) adv. very, with adjectives and verbs, ráb-tu (rab tu) sdoms (sdoms) lock (the door) well Dzl.; ráb-tu (rab tu) k'rós-par (khros par) gyúr-te (gyur te) Tar.; it occurs also in the following phrases: ráb-tu (rab tu) ₒbyín-pa ('byin pa) to receive or admit into a religious order, ráb-tu (rab tu) ₒbyúń-ba ('byung ba) to enter into a religious community, to take orders, slób-dpon (slob dpon) Čos-bańs-las (chos bangs las) being with, or being ordained by the teacher Chosbangs; rgyál-poi (rgyal poi) rigs-las (rigs las) (to take orders) as a descendant of the royal family, of the caste of noblemen Tar.; ráb-(tu) (rab tu) byúń(-ba) (byung ba) he that has taken orders, a novice, or in gen.: a clerical person; rab-byúń (rab byung) is also the name of the first year of the cycle of sixty years; rab-(tu) (rab tu) ynás(-par) (gnas par) byéd-pa (byed pa), mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) c. acc. or la (la), prop. 'to make firm or permanent', to consecrate, to hallow, a new house, esp. a temple, an idol; by this act a house is secured against accidents, and an idol is supposed to acquire life and to become the abode of the respective deity, which occasionally manifests itself by sundry miracles Glr.; ráb-tu (rab tu) ₒbyéd-pa ('byed pa) (also erron. byéd-pa (byed pa)) Cs. to analyze, but Tar. 96 it is equivalent to प्रकरण treatise, dissertation. rab-ₒbyáms-pa (rab 'byams pa) v. ₒbyáms-pa ('byams pa); rab-ₒóg (rab 'og) the second in rank, next in value, excellence etc., thus Dzl. 724, 5 (as a better reading for ₒbriń-mo ('bring mo)); rab-yáńs (rab yangs) very wide, very extensive Sch.; rab-ysál (rab gsal) 1. very clear, quite evident. 2. sbst. a small balcony or gallery, frequently seen in Tibetan houses. 3. Sch. history (?). II. also rabs (rabs), ford, rab-méd (rab med) without a ford, rab-só (rab so) = rabs (rabs) Sch.

col. also hrab-hrib (hrab hrib), mist, dimness, e. g. before the eyes, in consequence of impaired vision; *ko (kho) śrab-śrib (shrab shrib) máń-na (mang na) mi (mi) toń* (thong) he sees only a mist before his eyes, W.; skár-ma (skar ma) rab-ríb (rab rib) the faint glimmering of a star.

1. lineage, succession of families, race, family, rgyal-rábs (rgyal rabs) royal family or lineage, nobility; succession of kings; mi-rábs (mi rabs) human race; rabs-čád (rabs chad) a person whose lineage is broken off, i.e. childless, issueless, rabs-čád (rabs chad) bza-mí (bza' mi) ynyis (gnyis) a married couple without children Mil.; yá-rabs (ya rabs) the higher class of people, noblemen; má-rabs (ma rabs) the lower class, also: one belonging to the higher or lower class; collectively: rgán-rabs (rgan rabs) old men, aged people, yžón-rabs (gzhon rabs) youth, young persons; sńón-rabs (sngon rabs) the ancients (veteres), pyí-rabs (phyi rabs) men of modern times, descendants, posterity Glr., sńón-rabs-sgrúń (sngon rabs sgrung)
